title: 5 Opening or Closing an AgriInvest Account
body_text:
5	Opening or Closing an AgriInvest Account
5.1	Overview of Opening or Closing AgriInvest Accounts
Credit unions are responsible for opening an AgriInvest Account, but it is the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administration that determines a Participantâs eligibility. The AgriInvest Account Application only opens a Financial Institution account. It doesnât replace the AgriInvest Program application that Participants complete directly with AAFC Administration annually.
5.2	Opening an AgriInvest Account
To partake in the AgriInvest Program, new Participants must open an AgriInvest Account at a participating Financial Institution. A new Participant presents an AgriInvest Account Initiation Form to the Financial Institution to proceed with opening an account.
Joint accounts arenât permitted. The AgriInvest transit and account number must be unique to each Participant.
For partnerships, each partner must present their own AgriInvest Account Initiation Form to open separate accounts (if the partner wants to participate in the program).
A corporation, co-operative, or communal organization can open one AgriInvest Account for the entity.
To open an AgriInvest Account for a member, follow regular credit union procedures and ensure that the AgriInvest Account is
interest bearing,
fixed and identifiable (i.e., deposits canât be held in instruments with fluctuating values or in instruments that canât be immediately withdrawn),
available if a withdrawal is required.
Record the new account on the AgriInvest Activity Report. If a deposit is made at the same time, record this on a separate line. For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
5.3	Opening an AgriInvest Account to Receive a Transfer
Participants who want to transfer an AgriInvest Account to your Financial Institution must present an AgriInvest Account Transfer Form, completed and signed by the Participant to authorize all future deposits sent to this new AgriInvest Account.
Note:	An account transfer doesnât extend the deposit deadline. An account transfer should be requested well in advance of the deposit deadline to ensure that the new AgriInvest Account can be opened and the deposit posted on time.
To process a transfer:
Open an AgriInvest Account for the Participant per regular credit union procedures.
Record the new account on the AgriInvest Activity Report.
Send a request for transfer of funds to the Financial Institution holding the existing AgriInvest Account.
Record the transferred amount on the AgriInvest Activity File when the transfer is received from the other Financial Institution.
The deposit amount and the interest amount are entered on separate lines on the Activity File. For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
5.4	Closing an AgriInvest Account
A Participant can close the AgriInvest Account and withdraw the balance at any time. An account can also be closed with a request for transfer of AgriInvest funds by another Financial Institution. In addition, the Administration can send the Financial Institution a Letter of Direction to close an account if a Participant hasnât submitted an AgriInvest form to the Administration by the final filing deadline or has submitted forms reporting no sales or purchases of allowable commodities for two consecutive years.
Note:	It is recommended that transit and account numbers be closed for six months to one year before being reused for a different AgriInvest Participant.
To close the AgriInvest Account:
Pay out any remaining balances to the Participant or to the Financial Institution requesting the transfer of funds.
Record the withdrawn amount on the AgriInvest Activity File, and report the account as closed.
For more information on reporting account details, see Chapter 7, Reporting to Central 1.
5.5	Handling Estate Accounts
Donât freeze funds or process unauthorized withdrawals on an estate account. Advise the executor or trustee to contact AAFC Administration for details on handling an estate account. The Administration will send the Financial Institution a Letter of Direction to close the estateâs AgriInvest Account and transfer the funds to the surviving spouseâs AgriInvest Account.
5.6	Handling Divorce or Legal Separations
An AgriInvest Account can be divided into two individual accounts according to the terms of a separation/divorce settlement. Advise the Participants to contact AAFC Administration for details on how to proceed in the event of divorce or separation. The Administration will issue a letter to the Financial Institution holding the Participantâs account that is being divided, indicating the amount of funds to be transferred to the other Participantâs AgriInvest Account.
END
